references.text: Backer, CA. 1951. Elatinaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 4: 203-206
Leach, GJ 1989. Taxonomic revision of Bergia (Elatinaceae) in Australia. J. Adelaide Bot. Gard. 11: 75-100

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 31 March 2022

species.citation: B.Heyne ex Roth , Nov. Pl. Sp. (1821) 219 ; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 205.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, Australia, Bangladesh, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madura, Malay Peninsula, Middle East, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Vietnam. LUZON: Abra, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Pangasinan, Rizal, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Drier regions, in seasonally inundated sites like rice paddy fallows, also on roadsides, locally often very common. (?-)100m.

species.citation: Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 387 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 103. = Spergula serrata Blanco , Fl. Filip., 2nd Ed. (1845) 271. = Bergia glandulosa Turcz. , Bull. Soc. Imp. Naturalistes Moscou 27 (1854) 371 ; Rolfe, Journ. Bot. 23 (1885) 210; S.Vidal Phan. Cuming. Philip. (1885) 95; Rev. PI. Vase. Filip. (1886) 51. ≠ Bergia verticillata auct. non Willd. ; Fern.-Vill., Novis. App. (1880) 15.
species.distribution: China, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Bataan, Ilocos Norte, Rizal, Zambales.
species.notes: Leacg (1989) considers this species to be endemic to the Philippines. (?-)100m.
